patch.value: pm comments <ID> 'text' succeeds; --add remains supported; --author without explicit value does not break comment creation; docs and tests are updated.

Delivery feature under the comments UX hardening epic.

## Detailed implementation plan
1. Command UX wiring
   - Update comments command signature to accept optional positional text argument in addition to --add.
   - Allow --author optional value so bare --author falls back to PM_AUTHOR/settings/defaults instead of failing parse.
   - Add deterministic conflict handling when both positional text and --add are provided in one invocation.
2. Runtime behavior and compatibility
   - Preserve existing --add plain text, text=<value>, markdown text:, and stdin token - support.
   - Keep list mode behavior unchanged when neither positional text nor --add is provided.
3. Regression coverage
   - Add integration assertions for positional shorthand success.
   - Add coverage for conflict path and optional --author path using CLI invocation tests.
   - Keep help/runtime contract tests passing with updated command usage.
4. Documentation updates
   - Update README, PRD, AGENTS examples to show shorthand plus backward-compatible --add usage.
   - Update CHANGELOG Unreleased notes with compatibility-safe UX improvement.
5. Verification and closure
   - Run targeted tests, then full sandbox-safe test and coverage sweeps.
   - Record verification evidence in linked pm items and close/release epic-feature-task chain.

value.author: codex-agent
